text.Rotherham: And, though I morsel out all my goods,—and though I deliver up my body, that I may boast, and have not, love, I am profited, nothing.
text.KJV: 13:3 And though I bestowe all my goods to feede the poore, and though I giue my body to bee burned, and haue not charitie, it profiteth me nothing.
text.DRV: 13:3 And if I should distribute all my goods to be meat for the poor, and if I should deliver my body so that I burn, and have not charity, it doth profit me nothing.

text.NRSV: Love is patient; love is kind; love is not envious or boastful or arrogant
text.Rotherham: Love, is patient, is gracious. Love, is not envious, vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
text.KJV: 13:4 Charitie suffereth long, and is kinde: charitie enuieth not: charitie vaunteth not it selfe, is not puffed vp,
text.DRV: 13:4 Charity is patient, is benign: Charity envieth not, dealeth not perversely: is not puffed up,

text.NRSV: or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful;
text.Rotherham: Acteth not unbecomingly, seeketh not her own things, is not easily provoked, imputeth not that which is base,
text.KJV: 13:5 Doeth not behaue it selfe vnseemly, seeketh not her owne, is not easily prouoked, thinketh no euill,
text.DRV: 13:5 is not ambitious, seeketh not her own, is not provoked to anger, thinketh not evil,

text.NRSV: it does not rejoice in wrongdoing, but rejoices in the truth.
text.Rotherham: Rejoiceth not over unrighteousness, but rejoiceth in sympathy with truth,—
text.KJV: 13:6 Reioyceth not in iniquitie, but reioyceth in the trueth:
text.DRV: 13:6 rejoiceth not upon iniquity, but rejoiceth with the truth:

text.NRSV: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
text.Rotherham: All things, covereth, all things, believeth, all things, hopeth, all things, endureth.
text.KJV: 13:7 Beareth all things, beleeueth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
text.DRV: 13:7 suffereth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, beareth all things.

text.NRSV: Love never ends. But as for prophecies, they will come to an end; as for tongues, they will cease; as for knowledge, it will come to an end.
text.Rotherham: Love, at no time, faileth;—but, whether prophesyings, they shall be done away, whether tongues, they shall cease, whether gaining knowledge, it shall be done away;
text.KJV: 13:8 Charitie neuer faileth: but whether there be prophesies, they shall faile; whether there bee tongues, they shall cease; whether there bee knowledge, it shall vanish away.
text.DRV: 13:8 Charity never falleth away: whether prophecies shall be made void, or tongues shall cease, or knowledge shall be destroyed.

text.NRSV: For we know only in part, and we prophesy only in part;
text.Rotherham: For, in part, are we gaining knowledge, and, in part, are we prophesying,—
text.KJV: 13:9 For we know in part, and we prophesie in part.
text.DRV: 13:9 For in part we know, & in part we prophesy.

text.NRSV: but when the complete comes, the partial will come to an end.
text.Rotherham: But, as soon as that which is complete is come, that which is in part, shall be done away.
text.KJV: 13:10 But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part, shalbe done away.
text.DRV: 13:10 But when that shall come that is perfect, that shall be made void that is in part.

text.NRSV: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child; when I became an adult, I put an end to childish ways.
text.Rotherham: When I was a child, I used to speak as a child, to prefer as child, to reason as a child: now I have become a man, I have laid aside the things of the child!
text.KJV: 13:11 When I was a childe, I spake as a childe, I vnderstood as a childe, I thought as a childe: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
text.DRV: 13:11 When I was a little one, I spake as a little one, I understood as a little one, I thought as a little one. But when I was made a man, I did away the things that belonged to a little one.

text.NRSV: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then we will see face to face. Now I know only in part; then I will know fully, even as I have been fully known.
text.Rotherham: For we see, as yet, through a dim window, obscurely, but, then, face to face: as yet, I gain knowledge, in part, but, then, shall I fully know, even as I was also fully known.
text.KJV: 13:12 For now we see through a glasse, darkely: but then face to face: now I know in part, but then shall I know euen as also I am knowen.
text.DRV: 13:12 We see now by a glass in a dark sort: but then face to face. Now I know in part: but then I shall know as also I am known.

text.NRSV: And now faith, hope, and love abide, these three; and the greatest of these is love.
text.Rotherham: But, now abide—faith, hope, love,—these three; but, the greatest of these, is, love.
text.KJV: 13:13 And now abideth faith, hope, charitie, these three, but the greatest of these is charitie.
text.DRV: 13:13 And now there remain, faith, hope, charity, these three: but the greater of these is charity.

text.NRSV: Pursue love and strive for the spiritual gifts, and especially that you may prophesy.
text.Rotherham: Pursue love; nevertheless be envious of the spiritual gifts,—and, rather, that ye may be prophesying.
text.KJV: 14:1 Follow after charitie, and desire spirituall giftes, but rather that yee may prophesie.
text.DRV: 14:1 Follow Charity, earnestly pursue spiritual things: but rather that you may prophesy.

text.NRSV: For those who speak in a tongue do not speak to other people but to God; for no one understands them, since they are speaking mysteries in the Spirit.
text.Rotherham: For, he that speaketh with a tongue, not unto men, doth speak, but, unto God,—for, no one, understandeth, although, in spirit, he is speaking sacred secrets;
text.KJV: 14:2 For he that speaketh in an vnknowen tongue, speaketh not vnto men, but vnto God: for no man vnderstandeth him: howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.
text.DRV: 14:2 For he that speaketh with tongues speaketh not to men, but to God: for no man heareth. But in spirit he speaketh mysteries.

text.NRSV: On the other hand, those who prophesy speak to other people for their upbuilding and encouragement and consolation.
text.Rotherham: But, be that prophesieth, unto men, doth speak—edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
text.KJV: 14:3 But he that prophesieth, speaketh vnto men to edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
text.DRV: 14:3 For he that prophesieth, speaketh to men unto edification, & exhortation, & consolation.

text.NRSV: Those who speak in a tongue build up themselves, but those who prophesy build up the church.
text.Rotherham: He that speaketh with a tongue, buildeth up, himself, whereas, he that prophesieth, buildeth up, an assembly.
text.KJV: 14:4 He that speaketh in an vnknowen tongue, edifieth himselfe: but hee that prophesieth, edifieth the Church.
text.DRV: 14:4 He that speaketh with tongues, edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth, edifieth the Church.

text.NRSV: Now I would like all of you to speak in tongues, but even more to prophesy. One who prophesies is greater than one who speaks in tongues, unless someone interprets, so that the church may be built up.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I wish you all to speak with tongues, but, rather, that ye may prophesy,—moreover, greater, is he that prophesieth, than he that speaketh with tongues,—unless indeed he translate, that, the assembly, may receive upbuilding.
text.KJV: 14:5 I would that yee all spake with tongues, but rather that ye prophesied: for greater is hee that prophesieth, then hee that speaketh with tongues, except hee interprete, that the Church may receiue edifying.
text.DRV: 14:5 And I would have you all to speak with tongues, but rather to prophesy. For greater is he that prophesieth, than he that speaketh with tongues: unless perhaps he interpret, that the Church may take edification.

text.NRSV: Now, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I benefit you unless I speak to you in some revelation or knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text.Rotherham: But, now, brethren—if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit, you, except I speak, unto you, either by way of revelation, or knowledge, or prophesying, or teaching?
text.KJV: 14:6 Now brethren, if I come vnto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, except I shall speake to you either by reuelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?
text.DRV: 14:6 But now, brethren; if I come to you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, unless I speak to you either in revelation, or in knowledge, or in prophecy, or in doctrine?

text.NRSV: It is the same way with lifeless instruments that produce sound, such as the flute or the harp. If they do not give distinct notes, how will anyone know what is being played?
text.Rotherham: In like manner, the things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, if, a distinction in the sounds, they do not give, how shall it be known, what is being piped or harped?
text.KJV: 14:7 And euen things without life giuing sound, whether pipe or harpe, except they giue a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be knowen what is piped or harped?
text.DRV: 14:7 Yet the things without life that give a sound, be it pipe or harp, unless they give a distinction of sounds, how shall that be known which is piped, or which is harped?

text.NRSV: And if the bugle gives an indistinct sound, who will get ready for battle?
text.Rotherham: For, if also, an uncertain sound, a trumpet, should give, who shall prepare himself for battle?
text.KJV: 14:8 For if the trumpet giue an vncertaine sound, who shall prepare himselfe to the battell?
text.DRV: 14:8 For if the trumpet give an uncertain voice, who shall prepare himself to battle?

text.NRSV: So with yourselves; if in a tongue you utter speech that is not intelligible, how will anyone know what is being said? For you will be speaking into the air.
text.Rotherham: So, also, ye, through means of the tongue, except ye give intelligible discourse, how shall it be known what is being spoken? for ye will be speaking, to the air.
text.KJV: 14:9 So likewise you, except ye vtter by the tongue words easie to be vnderstood, how shall it be knowen what is spoken? for ye shall speake into the aire.
text.DRV: 14:9 So you also by a tongue unless you utter manifest speech, how shall that be known that is said? for you shall be speaking into the air.

text.NRSV: There are doubtless many different kinds of sounds in the world, and nothing is without sound.
text.Rotherham: There may happen to be so many kinds of languages in the world, and, not one, unspoken:—
text.KJV: 14:10 There are, it may bee, so many kindes of voices in the world, and none of them are without signification.
text.DRV: 14:10 There are (for example) so many kinds of tongues in this world, & none is without voice.

text.NRSV: If then I do not know the meaning of a sound, I will be a foreigner to the speaker and the speaker a foreigner to me.
text.Rotherham: If, then, I do not know the meaning of the language, I shall be, unto him that is speaking, a foreigner, and, he that is speaking, shall be, in my case, a foreigner.
text.KJV: 14:11 Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voyce, I shall bee vnto him that speaketh, a Barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a Barbarian vnto mee.
text.DRV: 14:11 If then I know not the virtue of the voice, I shall be to him to whom I speak, barbarous; and he that speaketh barbarous to me.

text.NRSV: So with yourselves; since you are eager for spiritual gifts, strive to excel in them for building up the church.
text.Rotherham: So, ye, also—since ye are, envious, of spirits, unto the upbuilding of the assembly, seek to be pre-eminent.
text.KJV: 14:12 Euen so ye, forasmuch as yee are zealous of spirituall gifts, seeke that yee may excell to the edifying of the Church.
text.DRV: 14:12 So you also, because you be emulators of spirits: seek to abound unto the edifying of the Church.

text.NRSV: Therefore, one who speaks in a tongue should pray for the power to interpret.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, he that speaketh with a tongue, let him pray that he may translate;
text.KJV: 14:13 Wherefore let him that speaketh in an vnknowen tongue, pray that he may interprete.
text.DRV: 14:13 And therefore he that speaketh with the tongue, let him pray that he may interpret.

text.NRSV: For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind is unproductive.
text.Rotherham: [For], if I am praying in a tongue, my spirit, is praying, but, my mind, is unfruitful.
text.KJV: 14:14 For if I pray in an vnknowen tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my vnderstanding is vnfruitfull.
text.DRV: 14:14 For if I pray with the tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is without fruit.

text.NRSV: What should I do then? I will pray with the spirit, but I will pray with the mind also; I will sing praise with the spirit, but I will sing praise with the mind also.
text.Rotherham: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, but I will pray also with the mind,—I will strike the strings with the spirit, [but] I will strike the strings also with the mind.
text.KJV: 14:15 What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and wil pray with vnderstanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the vnderstanding also.
text.DRV: 14:15 What is it then? I will pray in the spirit, I will pray also in the understanding: I will sing in the spirit, I will sing also in the understanding.

text.NRSV: Otherwise, if you say a blessing with the spirit, how can anyone in the position of an outsider say the “Amen” to your thanksgiving, since the outsider does not know what you are saying?
text.Rotherham: Else, if thou be blessing in a spirit, he that filleth up the place of the ungifted person, how shall he say the Amen upon thy thanksgiving? since indeed, what thou art saying, he knoweth not;
text.KJV: 14:16 Else, when thou shalt blesse with the spirit, how shall hee that occupieth the roome of the vnlearned, say Amen at thy giuing of thankes, seeing he vnderstandeth not what thou sayest?
text.DRV: 14:16 But if thou bless in the spirit, he that supplieth the place of the vulgar how shall he say, Amen, upon thy blessing? because he knoweth not what thou sayest.

text.NRSV: For you may give thanks well enough, but the other person is not built up.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, indeed, excellently art giving thanks, but, the other, is not being built up.
text.KJV: 14:17 For thou verily giuest thankes well: but the other is not edified.
text.DRV: 14:17 For thou indeed givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.

text.NRSV: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you;
text.Rotherham: I give thanks unto God!—More than ye all, am I speaking with tongues;
text.KJV: 14:18 I thanke my God, I speake with tongues more then you all.
text.DRV: 14:18 I give my God thanks, that I speak with the tongue of you all.

text.NRSV: nevertheless, in church I would rather speak five words with my mind, in order to instruct others, than ten thousand words in a tongue.
text.Rotherham: But, in assembly, I desire to speak five words with my mind, that, others also, I may instruct, than myriads of words in a tongue.
text.KJV: 14:19 Yet in the Church I had rather speake fiue words with my vnderstanding, that by my voyce I might teach others also, then ten thousand words in an vnknowen tongue.
text.DRV: 14:19 But in the Church I will speak five words with my understanding that I may instruct others also; rather than ten thousand words in a tongue.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, do not be children in your thinking; rather, be infants in evil, but in thinking be adults.
text.Rotherham: Brethren! do not become children, in your understandings; but, in baseness, become babes, while, in your understandings, ye become, full-grown.
text.KJV: 14:20 Brethren, bee not children in vnderstanding: how be it, in malice be yee children, but in vnderstanding be men.
text.DRV: 14:20 Brethren, be not made children in sense, but in malice be children, and in sense be perfect.

text.NRSV: In the law it is written, “By people of strange tongues and by the lips of foreigners I will speak to this people; yet even then they will not listen to me,” says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: In the law, it is written—With strange tongues, and with lips of strangers, will I speak unto this people; and, not even so, will they hearken unto me,—saith the Lord.
text.KJV: 14:21 In the Law it is written, With men of other tongues, and other lippes will I speake vnto this people: and yet for all that will they not heare me, saith the Lord.
text.DRV: 14:21 In the Law it is written: That in other tongues and other lips I will speak to this people: and neither so will they hear me, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Tongues, then, are a sign not for believers but for unbelievers, while prophecy is not for unbelievers but for believers.
text.Rotherham: So that, the tongues, are for a sign—not unto them that believe, but, unto them that believe not; whereas, prophesying, is not for them that believe not, but, for them that believe.
text.KJV: 14:22 Wherfore tongues are for a signe, not to them that beleeue, but to them that beleeue not: But prophesying serueth not for them that beleeue not, but for them which beleeue.
text.DRV: 14:22 Therefore languages are for a sign not to the faithful, but to infidels: but prophecies, not to infidels, but to the faithful.

text.NRSV: If, therefore, the whole church comes together and all speak in tongues, and outsiders or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are out of your mind?
text.Rotherham: If, then, the whole assembly come together with one consent, and, all, are speaking with tongues,—and there come in persons unskilled or unbelieving, will they not say that ye are raving?
text.KJV: 14:23 If therefore the whole Church be come together into some place, and all speake with tongues, & there come in those that are vnlearned, or vnbeleeuers, will they not say that ye are mad?
text.DRV: 14:23 If therefore the whole Church come together in one, and all speak with tongues, and there enter in vulgar persons or infidels, will they not say that you be mad?

text.NRSV: But if all prophesy, an unbeliever or outsider who enters is reproved by all and called to account by all.
text.Rotherham: But, if, all, be prophesying,—and there come in one who is unbelieving or unskilled, he is convicted by all, he is searched by all, the secrets of his heart, become manifest,
text.KJV: 14:24 But if all prophesie, and there come in one that beleeueth not, or one vnlearned: he is conuinced of all, he is iudged of all.
text.DRV: 14:24 But if all prophesy, and there enter in any infidel or vulgar person, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all.

text.NRSV: After the secrets of the unbeliever’s heart are disclosed, that person will bow down before God and worship him, declaring, “God is really among you.”
text.Rotherham: And, so, falling down upon his face, he will do homage unto God, reporting that, in reality, God is, among you.
text.KJV: 14:25 And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest, and so falling downe on his face, hee will worship God, and report that God is in you of a trueth.
text.DRV: 14:25 the secrets of his heart are made manifest, and so falling on his face he will adore God, pronouncing that God is in you indeed.

text.NRSV: What should be done then, brothers and sisters? When you come together, each one has a hymn, a lesson, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. Let all things be done for building up.
text.Rotherham: What, then, is it, brethren? Whensoever ye are coming together, each one, hath a psalm, hath a teaching, hath a revelation, hath a tongue, hath a translation:—let, all things, be done, unto building up.
text.KJV: 14:26 How is it then brethren? when ye come together, euery one of you hath a Psalme, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a reuelatiō, hath an interpretatiō: Let all things be done vnto edifying.
text.DRV: 14:26 What is it then, brethren? when you come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a revelation, hath a tongue, hath an interpretation: let all things be done to edification.

text.NRSV: If anyone speaks in a tongue, let there be only two or at most three, and each in turn; and let one interpret.
text.Rotherham: If, with a tongue, one is speaking, let it be by two, or, at the most, three,—and by turns; and let, one, be translating;
text.KJV: 14:27 If any man speake in an vnknowen tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course, and let one interprete.
text.DRV: 14:27 Whether a man speak with tongue, by two, or at the most by three, and in course, and let one interpret.

text.NRSV: But if there is no one to interpret, let them be silent in church and speak to themselves and to God.
text.Rotherham: But, if there be none to translate, let him keep silence in assembly, and, unto himself, be speaking, and, unto God!
text.KJV: 14:28 But if there be no interpreter, let him keepe silence in the Church, and let him speake to himselfe, and to God.
text.DRV: 14:28 But if there be not an interpreter, let him hold his peace in the Church, and speak to himself and to God.

text.NRSV: Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others weigh what is said.
text.Rotherham: Prophets, moreover, let two or three speak, and let, the others, judge.
text.KJV: 14:29 Let the Prophets speake two or three, and let the other iudge.
text.DRV: 14:29 And let Prophets speak two or three, and let the rest judge.

text.NRSV: If a revelation is made to someone else sitting nearby, let the first person be silent.
text.Rotherham: If, however, unto another, a revelation be made, as he is sitting, let the first be silent;
text.KJV: 14:30 If any thing be reueiled to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.
text.DRV: 14:30 But if it be revealed to another sitting, let the first hold his peace.

text.NRSV: For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all be encouraged.
text.Rotherham: For, one by one, ye can, all, be prophesying,—that, all, may learn, and, all, be encouraged.
text.KJV: 14:31 For yee may all prophesie one by one, that all may learne, and all may be comforted.
text.DRV: 14:31 For you may all prophesy one by one: that all may learn, and all may be exhorted:

text.NRSV: And the spirits of prophets are subject to the prophets,
text.Rotherham: And, spirits of prophets, unto prophets, do submit themselves;
text.KJV: 14:32 And the spirits of the Prophets are subiect to the Prophets.
text.DRV: 14:32 and the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.

text.NRSV: for God is a God not of disorder but of peace. (As in all the churches of the saints,
text.Rotherham: For God is not [a God] of confusion, but, of peace:—as in all the assemblies of the saints.
text.KJV: 14:33 For God is not the authour of confusion, but of peace, as in all Churches of the Saints.
text.DRV: 14:33 For God is not the God of dissension, but of peace: as also in all the Churches of the saints I teach.

text.NRSV: women should be silent in the churches. For they are not permitted to speak, but should be subordinate, as the law also says.
text.Rotherham: As for the women, in the assemblies, let them be silent, for it is not permitted them to be speaking; but let them be in submission,—even as, the law, saith.
text.KJV: 14:34 Let your women keepe silence in the Churches, for it is not permitted vnto them to speake; but they are commanded to bee vnder obedience: as also saith the Law.
text.DRV: 14:34 Let women hold their peace in the Churches: for it is not permitted them to speak, but to be subject, as also the Law saith.

text.NRSV: If there is anything they desire to know, let them ask their husbands at home. For it is shameful for a woman to speak in church.
text.Rotherham: If, however, they are wishing to learn something, at home, their own husbands, let them question; for it is a shame for a woman to be speaking in assembly.
text.KJV: 14:35 And if they will learne any thing, let them aske their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to speake in the Church.
text.DRV: 14:35 But if they list learn any thing, let them ask their own husbands at home. For it is a foul thing for a woman to speak in the Church.

text.NRSV: Or did the word of God originate with you? Or are you the only ones it has reached?)
text.Rotherham: Or, from you, did the word of God come forth? Or, unto you alone, did it extend?
text.KJV: 14:36 What? came the word of God out from you? or came it vnto you onely?
text.DRV: 14:36 Or did the word of God proceed from you? came it unto you only?

text.NRSV: Anyone who claims to be a prophet, or to have spiritual powers, must acknowledge that what I am writing to you is a command of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: If anyone thinketh himself to be a prophet, or spiritually gifted, let him acknowledge the things which I am writing to you,—that they are, a commandment, of the Lord.
text.KJV: 14:37 If any man thinke himselfe to be a Prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge, that the things that I write vnto you, are the commandements of the Lord.
text.DRV: 14:37 If any man seem to be a Prophet, or spiritual, let him know the things that I write to you, that they are the commandments of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Anyone who does not recognize this is not to be recognized.
text.Rotherham: But, if anyone knoweth not, he is unknown!
text.KJV: 14:38 But if any man bee ignorant, let him be ignorant.
text.DRV: 14:38 But if any man know not, he shall not be known.

text.NRSV: So, my friends, be eager to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues;
text.Rotherham: So, then, my brethren,—be zealous to prophesy, and do not forbid, to be speaking with tongues;
text.KJV: 14:39 Wherefore brethren, couet to prophesie, and forbid not to speake with tongues.
text.DRV: 14:39 Therefore, brethren, be earnest to prophesy: and to speak with tongues prohibit not.

text.NRSV: but all things should be done decently and in order.
text.Rotherham: But let, all things, with comeliness, and by arrangement, be done.
text.KJV: 14:40 Let all things be done decently, and in order.
text.DRV: 14:40 But let all things be done honestly and according to order among you.

text.NRSV: Now I would remind you, brothers and sisters, of the good news that I proclaimed to you, which you in turn received, in which also you stand,
text.Rotherham: Moreover, brethren, I make known unto you The joyful message, which I myself announced to you, which also ye received, in which also ye stand;
text.KJV: 15:1 Moreouer brethren, I declare vnto you the Gospel which I preached vnto you, which also you haue receiued, and wherein yee stand.
text.DRV: 15:1 And I do you to understand, brethren, the Gospel which I preached to you, which also you received, in the which also you stand,

text.NRSV: through which also you are being saved, if you hold firmly to the message that I proclaimed to you—unless you have come to believe in vain.
text.Rotherham: Through which also ye are being saved,—if ye hold fast, with what discourse, I announced the joyful message unto you; unless indeed, in vain, ye believed.
text.KJV: 15:2 By which also yee are saued, if yee keepe in memorie what I preached vnto you, vnlesse yee haue beleeued in vaine.
text.DRV: 15:2 by the which also you are saved, after what manner I preached unto you if you keep it, unless you have believed in vain.

text.NRSV: For I handed on to you as of first importance what I in turn had received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the scriptures,
text.Rotherham: For I delivered unto you, among the first things, what also I received:—how that Christ died for our sins, according to the Scriptures,
text.KJV: 15:3 For I deliuered vnto you first of all, that which I also receiued, how that Christ died for our sinnes according to the Scriptures:
text.DRV: 15:3 For I delivered unto you first of all which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures:

text.NRSV: and that he was buried, and that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the scriptures,
text.Rotherham: And that he was buried, and that he hath been raised, on the third day, according to the Scriptures,—
text.KJV: 15:4 And that he was buried, and that he rose againe the third day according to the Scriptures.
text.DRV: 15:4 and that he was buried, and that he rose again the third day, according to the Scriptures:

text.NRSV: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
text.Rotherham: And that he appeared unto Cephas, then, to the twelve,
text.KJV: 15:5 And that he was seene of Cephas, then of the twelue.
text.DRV: 15:5 and that he was seen of Cephas; and after that of the eleven.

text.NRSV: Then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have died.
text.Rotherham: After that, he appeared to above five hundred brethren at once,—of whom, the greater number, remain until even now, but, some, have fallen asleep,—
text.KJV: 15:6 And that hee was seene of aboue fiue hundred brethren at once: of whom the greater part remaine vnto this present, but some are fallen asleepe.
text.DRV: 15:6 Then was he seen of more than five hundred brethren together: of which many remain until this present, and some are a-sleep.

text.NRSV: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
text.Rotherham: After that, he appeared unto James, then, unto all the apostles,
text.KJV: 15:7 After that, he was seen of Iames, then of all the Apostles.
text.DRV: 15:7 Moreover he was seen of James, then of all the Apostles.

text.NRSV: Last of all, as to one untimely born, he appeared also to me.
text.Rotherham: And, last of all, just as if unto the unseasonable birth, he appeared, even unto me;
text.KJV: 15:8 And last of all he was seene of me also, as of one borne out of due time.
text.DRV: 15:8 And last of all, as it were of an abortive he was seen also of me.

text.NRSV: For I am the least of the apostles, unfit to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
text.Rotherham: For, I, am the least of the apostles, who am not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.
text.KJV: 15:9 For I am the least of the Apostles, that am not meet to be called an Apostle because I persecuted þ Church of God.
text.DRV: 15:9 For I am the least of the Apostles, who am not worthy to be called an Apostle, because I persecuted the Church of God.

text.NRSV: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me has not been in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them—though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me.
text.Rotherham: But, by favour of God, I am what I am, and, his favour, which was unto me, hath not been made void,—but, much more abundantly than they all, have I toiled, albeit not, I, but the favour of God with me.
text.KJV: 15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed vpō me, was not in vaine: But I laboured more abundantly then they all, yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me:
text.DRV: 15:10 But by the grace of God I am that which I am; & his grace in me hath not been void, but I have laboured more aboundantly than all they: yet not I, but the grace of God with me.

text.NRSV: Whether then it was I or they, so we proclaim and so you have come to believe.
text.Rotherham: Whether therefore, I, or, they, thus do we proclaim, and, thus did ye believe.
text.KJV: 15:11 Therefore, whether it were I or they, so we preach, aud so ye beleeued.
text.DRV: 15:11 For whether I, or they, so we preach, and so you have believed.

text.NRSV: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, how can some of you say there is no resurrection of the dead?
text.Rotherham: Now, if, Christ is proclaimed, that, from among the dead, he hath been raised, how say some, among you—resurrection of the dead, there is none?
text.KJV: 15:12 Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you, that there is no resurrection of the dead?
text.DRV: 15:12 But if Christ be preached that he is risen again from the dead, how do certain among you say, that there is no resurrection of the dead?

text.NRSV: If there is no resurrection of the dead, then Christ has not been raised;
text.Rotherham: But, if, resurrection of the dead, there is none, not even Christ, hath been raised;
text.KJV: 15:13 But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen.
text.DRV: 15:13 And if there be no resurrection of the dead, neither is Christ risen again.

text.NRSV: and if Christ has not been raised, then our proclamation has been in vain and your faith has been in vain.
text.Rotherham: And, if Christ, hath not been raised, void, after all, is our proclamation, void also, our faith,—
text.KJV: 15:14 And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vaine, and your faith is also vaine:
text.DRV: 15:14 And if Christ be not risen again, then vain is our preaching, vain also is your faith.

text.NRSV: We are even found to be misrepresenting God, because we testified of God that he raised Christ—whom he did not raise if it is true that the dead are not raised.
text.Rotherham: And we are found, even false-witnesses of God, because we have witnessed respecting God, that he raised the Christ,—whom he did not raise, if, indeed, after all, the dead are not raised!
text.KJV: 15:15 Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God, because we haue testified of God, that he raised vp Christ: whom hee raised not vp, if so bee that the dead rise not.
text.DRV: 15:15 And we are found also false witnesses of God: because we have given testimony against God, that he hath raised up Christ, whom he hath not raised up, if the dead rise not again.

text.NRSV: For if the dead are not raised, then Christ has not been raised.
text.Rotherham: For, if the dead are not raised, not even Christ, hath been raised;
text.KJV: 15:16 For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised.
text.DRV: 15:16 For if the dead rise not again, neither is Christ risen again.

text.NRSV: If Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile and you are still in your sins.
text.Rotherham: And, if Christ hath not been raised, to no purpose, is your faith, yet, are ye in your sins!
text.KJV: 15:17 And if Christ be not raised, your faith is vaine, ye are yet in your sinnes.
text.DRV: 15:17 And if Christ be not risen again, vain is your faith, for yet you are in your sins.

text.NRSV: Then those also who have died in Christ have perished.
text.Rotherham: Hence also, they who are fallen asleep in Christ, are lost:
text.KJV: 15:18 Then they also which are fallen asleepe in Christ, are perished.
text.DRV: 15:18 Then they also that are a-sleep in Christ, are perished.

text.NRSV: If for this life only we have hoped in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied.
text.Rotherham: If, in this life, in Christ, we have hoped—and that is all, we are, of all men, most to be pitied.
text.KJV: 15:19 If in this life only we haue hope in Christ, wee are of all men most miserable.
text.DRV: 15:19 If in this life only we be hoping in Christ, we are more miserable than all men.

text.NRSV: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead, the first fruits of those who have died.
text.Rotherham: But, now, hath Christ been raised from among the dead,—a firstfruit of them who have fallen asleep;
text.KJV: 15:20 But now is Christ risen from the dead, and become the first fruits of them that slept.
text.DRV: 15:20 But now Christ is risen again from the dead, the first fruits of them that sleep:

text.NRSV: For since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead has also come through a human being;
text.Rotherham: For, since indeed, through a man, came death, through a man, also cometh the raising of the dead;
text.KJV: 15:21 For since by man came death, by man came also the resurrection of the dead.
text.DRV: 15:21 for by a man death; and by a man the resurrection of the dead.

text.NRSV: for as all die in Adam, so all will be made alive in Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, just as, in the Adam, all die, so, also, in the Christ, shall all be made alive.
text.KJV: 15:22 For as in Adam all die, euen so in Christ shall all be made aliue.
text.DRV: 15:22 And as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all shall be made alive.

text.NRSV: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then at his coming those who belong to Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, each, in his own rank:—A firstfruit, Christ, after that, they who are the Christ’s, in his presence,
text.KJV: 15:23 But euery man in his owne order. Christ the first fruits, afterward they that are Christs, at his comming.
text.DRV: 15:23 But every one in his own order: the first fruits Christ, then they that are of Christ, that believed in his coming.

text.NRSV: Then comes the end, when he hands over the kingdom to God the Father, after he has destroyed every ruler and every authority and power.
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, the end—whensoever he delivereth up the kingdom unto his God and Father, whensoever he shall bring to nought all rule and all authority and power;
text.KJV: 15:24 Then commeth the end, when he shall haue deliuered vp the kingdome to God euen the Father, when he shall haue put downe all rule, and all authority and power.
text.DRV: 15:24 Then the end, when he shall have delivered the Kingdom to God and the Father, when he shall have abolished all principality & authority & power.

text.NRSV: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
text.Rotherham: For he must needs reign, until he shall put all his enemies under his feet:
text.KJV: 15:25 For he must reigne, till hee hath put all enemies vnder his feete.
text.DRV: 15:25 And he must reign, Until he put all his enemies under his feet.

text.NRSV: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
text.Rotherham: As a last enemy, death, is to be destroyed;
text.KJV: 15:26 The last enemie that shall be destroyed, is death.
text.DRV: 15:26 And the enemy death shall be destroyed last. For he hath subdued all things under his feet. And whereas he saith,

text.NRSV: For “God has put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when it says, “all things are put in subjection,” it is plain that this does not include the one who put all things in subjection under him.
text.Rotherham: For—He put, all things, in subjection under his feet. But, whensoever it shall be said—all things are in subjection!—it is evident that it means,—Except him who did put into subjection, unto him, the all things—
text.KJV: 15:27 For he hath put all things vnder his feete; but when hee saith all things are put vnder him, it is manifest that he is excepted which did put all things vnder him.
text.DRV: 15:27 All things are subdued to him; undoubtedly, except him that subdued all things unto him.

text.NRSV: When all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself will also be subjected to the one who put all things in subjection under him, so that God may be all in all.
text.Rotherham: But whensoever have been put into subjection, unto him, the all things, then, the Son himself, [also] shall be put in subjection unto him who put in subjection, unto him, the all things,—that, God, may be, all things in all.
text.KJV: 15:28 And when all things shall bee subdued vnto him, then shal the Sonne also himselfe bee subiect vnto him that put all things vnder him, that God may be all in all.
text.DRV: 15:28 And when all things shall be subdued to him; then the Son also himself shall be subject to him that subdued all things unto him, that God may be all in all.

text.NRSV: Otherwise, what will those people do who receive baptism on behalf of the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized on their behalf?
text.Rotherham: Else, what will they do, who are being immersed in behalf of the dead? If, not at all, are the dead to be raised, why are they even being immersed in their behalf?
text.KJV: 15:29 Else what shal they do, which are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not at all, why are they then baptized for the dead?
text.DRV: 15:29 Otherwise what shall they do that are baptized for the dead, if the dead rise not again at all?

text.NRSV: And why are we putting ourselves in danger every hour?
text.Rotherham: Why also are, we, running into peril every hour?
text.KJV: 15:30 And why stand we in ieopardy euery houre?
text.DRV: 15:30 Why also are they baptized for them? Why also are we in danger every hour?

text.NRSV: I die every day! That is as certain, brothers and sisters, as my boasting of you—a boast that I make in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Day by day, am I dying!—Yea! by your own boasting, brethren, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.KJV: 15:31 I protest by your reioycing which I haue in Christ Iesus our Lord, I die dayly.
text.DRV: 15:31 I die daily by your glory, brethren, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: If with merely human hopes I fought with wild animals at Ephesus, what would I have gained by it? If the dead are not raised, “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
text.Rotherham: If, after the manner of men, I have fought with wild-beasts at Ephesus, what, to me, the profit? If the dead are not raised, Let us eat and drink, for, tomorrow, we die.
text.KJV: 15:32 If after the maner of men I haue fought with beasts at Ephesus, what aduantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let vs eate and drinke, for to morrowe wee die.
text.DRV: 15:32 If (according to man) I fought with beasts at Ephesus, what doth it profit me, if the dead rise not again? Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we shall die.

text.NRSV: Do not be deceived: “Bad company ruins good morals.”
text.Rotherham: Be not deceiving yourselves,—evil communications corrupt gentle manners:—
text.KJV: 15:33 Bee not deceiued: euill communications corrupt good manners.
text.DRV: 15:33 Be not seduced, Evil communications corrupt good manners.

text.NRSV: Come to a sober and right mind, and sin no more; for some people have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
text.Rotherham: Wake up to sobriety, in righteousness, and be not committing sin; for some have, an ignorance of God: for shame, unto you, am I speaking!
text.KJV: 15:34 Awake to righteousnesse, and sinne not: for some haue not the knowledge of God, I speake this to your shame.
text.DRV: 15:34 Awake ye just, and sin not. For some have not the knowledge of God, I speak to your shame.

text.NRSV: But someone will ask, “How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?”
text.Rotherham: But some one will say—How, are the dead raised? and, with what kind of body, do they come?
text.KJV: 15:35 But some man will say, How are the dead raysed vp? and with what body doe they come?
text.DRV: 15:35 But some man saith: How do the dead rise again? and with what manner of body shall they come?

text.NRSV: Fool! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
text.Rotherham: Simple one! What, thou, sowest, is not quickened, except it die;
text.KJV: 15:36 Thou foole, that which thou sowest, is not quickened except it die.
text.DRV: 15:36 Fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, unless it die first.

text.NRSV: And as for what you sow, you do not sow the body that is to be, but a bare seed, perhaps of wheat or of some other grain.
text.Rotherham: And, what thou sowest, not the body that shall come into existence, dost thou sow, but a naked kernel—if it so happen, of wheat, or of any of the rest,—
text.KJV: 15:37 And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare graine, it may chance of wheate, or of some other graine.
text.DRV: 15:37 And that which thou sowest, not the body that shall be, doest thou sow; but bare grain, to wit, of wheat, or of some of the rest.

text.NRSV: But God gives it a body as he has chosen, and to each kind of seed its own body.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, God, giveth it a body, as he pleased, and, unto each of the seeds, a body of its own.
text.KJV: 15:38 But God giueth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to euery seed his owne body.
text.DRV: 15:38 And God giveth it a body as he will: & to every seed his proper body.

text.NRSV: Not all flesh is alike, but there is one flesh for human beings, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
text.Rotherham: Not all flesh, is the same flesh; but, one, indeed, is, [the flesh] of men, and, another, the flesh of beasts, and, another, the flesh of birds, and, another, of fishes;
text.KJV: 15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, and another of birds.
text.DRV: 15:39 Not all flesh, is the same flesh: but one of men, another of beasts, another of birds, another of fishes.

text.NRSV: There are terrestrial bodies and there are celestial bodies, but the glory of the celestial is one, and the glory of the terrestrial is another.
text.Rotherham: And there are heavenly bodies, and earthly bodies,—but, of one kind, indeed, is the glory of the heavenly, and, of another kind, is the glory of the earthly;—
text.KJV: 15:40 There are also celestiall bodies, and bodies terrestriall: But the glorie of the celestiall is one, and the glorie of the terrestriall is another.
text.DRV: 15:40 And bodies celestial, & bodies terrestrial: but, one glory of the celestial, and another of the terrestrial.

text.NRSV: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; indeed, star differs from star in glory.
text.Rotherham: One, is the glory of a sun, and, another, the glory of a moon, and, another, the glory of stars,—nay! star from star, differeth in glory.
text.KJV: 15:41 There is one glory of the sunne, another of the moone, and another glorie of the starres: for one starre differeth from another starre in glorie.
text.DRV: 15:41 One glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars. For star differeth from star in glory:

text.NRSV: So it is with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable, what is raised is imperishable.
text.Rotherham: Thus, also the resurrection of the dead: it is sown in corruption, it is raised in incorruption,
text.KJV: 15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead, it is sowen in corruption, it is raised in incorruption.
text.DRV: 15:42 so also the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption, it shall rise in incorruption.

text.NRSV: It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.
text.Rotherham: It is sown in dishonour, it is raised in glory, it is sown in weakness, it is raised in power,
text.KJV: 15:43 It is sowen in dishonour, it is raysed in glorie: it is sowen in weakenesse, it is raysed in power:
text.DRV: 15:43 It is sown in dishonour, it shall rise in glory. It is sown in infirmity, it shall rise in power.

text.NRSV: It is sown a physical body, it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a physical body, there is also a spiritual body.
text.Rotherham: It is sown a body of the soul, it is raised a body of the spirit; if there is a body of the soul, there is also of the spirit:—
text.KJV: 15:44 It is sowen a naturall body, it is raised a spirituall bodie. There is a naturall bodie, and there is a spirituall bodie.
text.DRV: 15:44 It is sown a natural body; it shall rise a spiritual body. If there be a natural body, there is also a spiritual,

text.NRSV: Thus it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living being”; the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
text.Rotherham: Thus, also, it is written—The first man, Adam, became, a living soul, the last Adam, a life-giving spirit.
text.KJV: 15:45 And so it is written: The first man Adam was made a liuing soule, the last Adam was made a quickening spirit.
text.DRV: 15:45 as it is written: The first man Adam was made into a living soul: the last Adam into a quickening spirit.

text.NRSV: But it is not the spiritual that is first, but the physical, and then the spiritual.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, not first, is the [body] of the spirit, but that, of the soul,—afterwards, that of the spirit.
text.KJV: 15:46 Howbeit that was not first which is spirituall: but that which is naturall, and afterward that which is spirituall.
text.DRV: 15:46 Yet that is not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural: afterward that which is spiritual.

text.NRSV: The first man was from the earth, a man of dust; the second man is from heaven.
text.Rotherham: The first man, is of the ground, earthy, the second man, is, of heaven:
text.KJV: 15:47 The first man is of the earth, earthy: The second man is the Lord from heauen.
text.DRV: 15:47 The first man of earth, earthly: the second man from Heaven, heavenly.

text.NRSV: As was the man of dust, so are those who are of the dust; and as is the man of heaven, so are those who are of heaven.
text.Rotherham: As, the man of earth, such, also, the men of earth, and, as, the man of heaven, such, also, the men of heaven;
text.KJV: 15:48 As is the earthy, such are they that are earthy, and as is the heauenly, such are they also that are heauenly.
text.DRV: 15:48 Such as is the earthly, such also are the earthly: and such as the heavenly, such also are the heavenly.

text.NRSV: Just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we will also bear the image of the man of heaven.
text.Rotherham: And, even as we have borne the image of the man of earth, let us also bear the image of the man of heaven.
text.KJV: 15:49 And as we haue borne the image of the earthy, wee shall also beare the image of the heauenly.
text.DRV: 15:49 Therefore as we have borne the image of the earthly, let us bear also the image of the heavenly.

text.NRSV: What I am saying, brothers and sisters, is this: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
text.Rotherham: And, this, I say, brethren,—that, flesh and blood, cannot inherit, God’s kingdom. Neither doth, corruption, inherit, incorruption.
text.KJV: 15:50 Now this I say, brethren, that flesh & blood cannot inherite the kingdome of God: neither doth corruption inherite incorruption.
text.DRV: 15:50 This I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot possess the Kingdom of God: neither shall corruption possess incorruption.

text.NRSV: Listen, I will tell you a mystery! We will not all die, but we will all be changed,
text.Rotherham: Lo! a sacred secret, unto you, do I declare:—we shall not, all, sleep, but we shall, all, be changed,—
text.KJV: 15:51 Behold, I shew you a mysterie: we shall not all sleepe, but wee shall all be changed,
text.DRV: 15:51 Behold I tell you a mystery. We shall all indeed rise again: but we shall not all be changed.

text.NRSV: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we will be changed.
text.Rotherham: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, during the last trumpet; for it shall sound, and, the dead, shall be raised, incorruptible, and, we, shall be changed.
text.KJV: 15:52 In a moment, in the twinckling of an eye, at the last trumpe, (for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.)
text.DRV: 15:52 In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet (for the trumpet shall sound) and the dead shall rise again incorruptible: and we shall be changed.

text.NRSV: For this perishable body must put on imperishability, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
text.Rotherham: For this corruptible must needs clothe itself with incorruptibility, and this mortal, clothe itself, with immortality.
text.KJV: 15:53 For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortall must put on immortalitie.
text.DRV: 15:53 For this corruptible must do on incorruption; & this mortal do on immortality.

text.NRSV: When this perishable body puts on imperishability, and this mortal body puts on immortality, then the saying that is written will be fulfilled: “Death has been swallowed up in victory.”
text.Rotherham: But, whensoever, this mortal, shall clothe itself with immortality, then, shall be brought to pass the saying that is written—Death hath been swallowed up, victoriously;
text.KJV: 15:54 So when this corruptible shall haue put on incorruption, & this mortall shall haue put on immortality, then shall be brought to passe the saying that is written, Death is swallowed vp in victorie.
text.DRV: 15:54 And when this mortal hath done on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: Death is swallowed up in victory.

text.NRSV: “Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?”
text.Rotherham: Where, O death, is thy victory? Where, O death, is thy sting?
text.KJV: 15:55 O death, where is thy sting? O graue, where is thy victorie?
text.DRV: 15:55 Death where is thy victory? Death where is thy sting?

text.NRSV: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sting of death, is, sin, and, the power of sin, is, the law;—
text.KJV: 15:56 The sting of death is sinne, and the strength of sinne is the law.
text.DRV: 15:56 And the sting of death, is sin: and the power of sin is the Law.

text.NRSV: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, unto God, be thanks, who is giving unto us the victory, through our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 15:57 But thankes bee to God, which giueth vs the victorie, through our Lord Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 15:57 But thanks be to God that hath given us the victory by our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my beloved, be steadfast, immovable, always excelling in the work of the Lord, because you know that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.
text.Rotherham: So, then, my beloved brethren,—become ye, steadfast, immovable, superabounding in the work of the Lord, at all times; knowing that, your toil, is not in vain in the Lord.
text.KJV: 15:58 Therefore my beloued brethren, be yee stedfast, vnmoueable, alwayes abounding in the worke of the Lord, forasmuch as you know that your labour is not in vaine in the Lord.
text.DRV: 15:58 Therefore, my beloved brethren, be stable and unmoveable; abounding in the work of our Lord always, knowing that your labour is not vain in our Lord:

text.NRSV: Now concerning the collection for the saints: you should follow the directions I gave to the churches of Galatia.
text.Rotherham: Now, concerning the collection which is for the saints, just as I directed the assemblies of Galatia, so, also do, ye:—
text.KJV: 16:1 Now concerning the collection for the Saints, as I haue giuen order to the Churches of Galatia, euen so doe ye.
text.DRV: 16:1 And concerning the collections that are made for the Saints, as I have ordained to the Churches of Galatia, so do ye also.

text.NRSV: On the first day of every week, each of you is to put aside and save whatever extra you earn, so that collections need not be taken when I come.
text.Rotherham: Upon the first of the week, let, each one of you, put, by itself, in store, as he may be prospering,—lest, as soon as I come, then, collections, should be in progress.
text.KJV: 16:2 Upon the first day of the weeke, let euery one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.
text.DRV: 16:2 In the first of the Sabboth let every one of you put a part with himself, laying up what shall well like him: that not when I come, then collections be made.

text.NRSV: And when I arrive, I will send any whom you approve with letters to take your gift to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, as soon as I arrive, whomsoever ye shall approve by letters, these, will I send, to bear away your favour unto Jerusalem:
text.KJV: 16:3 And when I come, whomsoeuer you shall approue by your letters, them wil I send to bring your liberality vnto Ierusalem.
text.DRV: 16:3 And when I shall be present; whom you shall approve by letters, them will I send to carry your grace into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: If it seems advisable that I should go also, they will accompany me.
text.Rotherham: And, if it be meet that, I also, be journeying, with me, shall they journey.
text.KJV: 16:4 And if it be meet that I goe also, they shall goe with me.
text.DRV: 16:4 And if it be worthy that I also go, they shall go with me.

text.NRSV: I will visit you after passing through Macedonia—for I intend to pass through Macedonia—
text.Rotherham: Now I will come unto you, as soon as I have passed through Macedonia,—for I do pass through Macedonia,
text.KJV: 16:5 Now I wil come vnto you, when I shall passe through Macedonia: for I doe passe through Macedonia.
text.DRV: 16:5 And I will come to you, when I shall have passed through Macedonia. For I will pass through Macedonia.

text.NRSV: and perhaps I will stay with you or even spend the winter, so that you may send me on my way, wherever I go.
text.Rotherham: And, with you, I may perhaps sojourn, or winter, that, ye, may set me forward, whithersoever I may be journeying.
text.KJV: 16:6 And it may bee that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that yee may bring me on my iourny, whithersoeuer I goe.
text.DRV: 16:6 And with you perhaps I will abide, or will winter also: that you may bring me on my way whithersoever I go.

text.NRSV: I do not want to see you now just in passing, for I hope to spend some time with you, if the Lord permits.
text.Rotherham: For I do not wish to see you, just now, by the way; for I hope to remain, some time, with you,—if, the Lord, permit.
text.KJV: 16:7 For I will not see you now by the way, but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
text.DRV: 16:7 For I will not now see you by the way, for I hope that I shall abide with you some little time, if our Lord will permit.

text.NRSV: But I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
text.Rotherham: But I remain in Ephesus, until the Pentecost,
text.KJV: 16:8 But I will tarry at Ephesus vntill Pentecost.
text.DRV: 16:8 But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.

text.NRSV: for a wide door for effective work has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.
text.Rotherham: For, a door, unto me, hath opened, great and effectual, and, opposers, are many.
text.KJV: 16:9 For a great doore and effectuall is opened vnto mee, and there are many aduersaries.
text.DRV: 16:9 For a great door and evident is opened unto me: and many adversaries.

text.NRSV: If Timothy comes, see that he has nothing to fear among you, for he is doing the work of the Lord just as I am;
text.Rotherham: But, if Timothy should come, see that, without fear, he be with you, for, in the work of the Lord, doth he labour, even as, I:
text.KJV: 16:10 Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without feare: for hee worketh the worke of the Lord, as I also doe.
text.DRV: 16:10 And if Timothee come, see that he be without fear with you, for he worketh the work of our Lord, as also I.

text.NRSV: therefore let no one despise him. Send him on his way in peace, so that he may come to me; for I am expecting him with the brothers.
text.Rotherham: Let no one then despise him; and set ye him forward in peace, that he may come unto me, for I expect him with the brethren.
text.KJV: 16:11 Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that hee may come vnto me: for I looke for him with the brethren.
text.DRV: 16:11 Let no man therefore despise him, but conduct ye him in peace: that he may come to me. For I expect him with the brethren.

text.NRSV: Now concerning our brother Apollos, I strongly urged him to visit you with the other brothers, but he was not at all willing to come now. He will come when he has the opportunity.
text.Rotherham: But, concerning Apollos the brother, much, did I beseech him, that he would come unto you, with the brethren, but there was, by no means, any will, that he should come, now;—he will come, however, as soon as he hath good opportunity.
text.KJV: 16:12 As touching our brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come vnto you with þ brethren, but his wil was not at all to come at this time: but he wil come when hee shall haue conuenient time.
text.DRV: 16:12 And of brother Apollo I do you to understand, that I much entreated him to come unto you with the brethren: and at all it was not his mind to come now. But he will come when he shall have leisure.

text.NRSV: Keep alert, stand firm in your faith, be courageous, be strong.
text.Rotherham: Be on the watch, stand firm in the faith, be men—be strong;
text.KJV: 16:13 Watch yee, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men: be strong.
text.DRV: 16:13 Watch ye, stand in the faith, do manfully, & be strengthened.

text.NRSV: Let all that you do be done in love.
text.Rotherham: Let, all your affairs, in love, be carried on.
text.KJV: 16:14 Let all your things be done with charitie.
text.DRV: 16:14 Let all your things be done in charity.

text.NRSV: Now, brothers and sisters, you know that members of the household of Stephanas were the first converts in Achaia, and they have devoted themselves to the service of the saints;
text.Rotherham: Now I beseech you, brethren,—ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is a first-fruit of Achaia, and, for the purpose of ministering, they devoted themselves, unto the saints—
text.KJV: 16:15 I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the first fruits of Achaia, and that they haue addicted themselues to the ministery of the Saints,)
text.DRV: 16:15 And I beseech you, brethren, you know the house of Stephanas, and of Fortunatus, that they are the first fruits of Achaia, & have ordained themselves to the ministry of the saints:

text.NRSV: I urge you to put yourselves at the service of such people, and of everyone who works and labors with them.
text.Rotherham: That, ye also, be submitting yourselves unto such as these,—and unto everyone helping in the work and toiling.
text.KJV: 16:16 That ye submit your selues vnto such, and to euery one that helpeth with vs and laboureth.
text.DRV: 16:16 that you also be subject to such, and to every one that helpeth & laboureth with us.

text.NRSV: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because they have made up for your absence;
text.Rotherham: I Rejoice, moreover, in the presence of Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus: because, your own shortcoming, these, have filled up:—
text.KJV: 16:17 I am glad of the comming of Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part, they haue supplied.
text.DRV: 16:17 And I rejoice in the presence of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because that which you wanted, they have supplied.

text.NRSV: for they refreshed my spirit as well as yours. So give recognition to such persons.
text.Rotherham: They have given rest, in fact, unto my spirit, and yours: hold in acknowledgment, therefore, such as these.
text.KJV: 16:18 For they haue refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge yee them that are such.
text.DRV: 16:18 For they have refreshed both my spirit and yours. Know them therefore that are such.

text.NRSV: The churches of Asia send greetings. Aquila and Prisca, together with the church in their house, send greetings in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The assemblies of Asia salute you: Aquila and Priscilla, with the assembly meeting at their house, salute you much in the Lord:
text.KJV: 16:19 The Churches of Asia salute you: Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the Church that is in their house.
text.DRV: 16:19 The Churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla with their domestical Church salute you much in our Lord.

text.NRSV: All the brothers and sisters send greetings. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
text.Rotherham: All the brethren salute you:—Salute ye one another with a holy kiss.
text.KJV: 16:20 All the brethren greet you: greet ye one another with an holy kisse.
text.DRV: 16:20 All the brethren salute you. Salute one another in a holy kiss.

text.NRSV: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand.
text.Rotherham: The salutation of Paul—with my own hand.
text.KJV: 16:21 The salutation of me Paul, with mine owne hand.
text.DRV: 16:21 The salutation with mine own hand Paul's.

text.NRSV: Let anyone be accursed who has no love for the Lord. Our Lord, come!
text.Rotherham: If anyone doth not dearly love the Lord, let him be anathema [that is, "accursed"] : Maran atha [that is, "The Lord, cometh"] .
text.KJV: 16:22 If any man loue not the Lord Iesus Christ, let him bee Anathema Maranatha.
text.DRV: 16:22 If any man love not our Lord Jesus Christ, be he anathema. Maran atha.

text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
text.Rotherham: The favour of the Lord Jesus, be with you.
text.KJV: 16:23 The grace of our Lord Iesus Christ be with you.
text.DRV: 16:23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

text.NRSV: My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.
text.Rotherham: My love, be with you all, in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: 16:24 My loue be with you all in Christ Iesus, Amen. The first Epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi by Stephanas, and Fortunatus, and Achaicus, and Timotheus.
text.DRV: 16:24 My charity be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
